#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2025 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# Bash Utility Regime Dispatch - Direct bash dispatch

set -euo pipefail

BURE_VERBOSE=${BURE_VERBOSE:-0}

BURD_REGIME_FILE=${BURD_REGIME_FILE:-"__MISSING_BURD_REGIME_FILE__"}

# Utility function for verbose output
zbud_show() { test "$BURE_VERBOSE" != "1" || echo "BURDSHOW: $*"; }

# Enable trace mode if verbose level is 2
if test "${BURE_VERBOSE}" = "2"; then
  set -x
fi

zbud_die() { echo "FATAL: $*" >&2; exit 1; }

zburd_sentinel() {
  test "${ZBURD_INITIALIZED:-}" = "1" || zbud_die "Dispatch not initialized - zbud_main not complete"
}

# String validator with optional length constraints
zbud_check_string() {
  local -r z_context="${1}"
  local -r z_varname="${2}"
  local z_val="${!z_varname:-}"
  local -r z_min="${3}"
  local -r z_max="${4}"

  test "${z_min}" != "0" -o -n "${z_val}" || return 0
  test -n "${z_val}" || zbud_die "[${z_context}] ${z_varname} must not be empty"

  if test -n "${z_max}"; then
    test ${#z_val} -ge "${z_min}" || zbud_die "[${z_context}] ${z_varname} must be at least ${z_min} chars, got '${z_val}' (${#z_val})"
    test ${#z_val} -le "${z_max}" || zbud_die "[${z_context}] ${z_varname} must be no more than ${z_max} chars, got '${z_val}' (${#z_val})"
  fi
}

# Source configuration and setup environment
zbud_setup() {
  zbud_show "Starting BDU setup"

  source            "${BURD_REGIME_FILE}"

  # Apply BURV (Bash Utility Regime Verification) overrides if set
  BURC_OUTPUT_ROOT_DIR="${BURV_OUTPUT_ROOT_DIR:-${BURC_OUTPUT_ROOT_DIR}}"
  BURC_TEMP_ROOT_DIR="${BURV_TEMP_ROOT_DIR:-${BURC_TEMP_ROOT_DIR}}"

  zbud_check_string "${BURD_REGIME_FILE}" BURC_STATION_FILE        1 256
  zbud_check_string "${BURD_REGIME_FILE}" BURC_LOG_LAST            1 256
  zbud_check_string "${BURD_REGIME_FILE}" BURC_LOG_EXT             1 32
  zbud_check_string "${BURD_REGIME_FILE}" BURC_TABTARGET_DIR       1 256
  zbud_check_string "${BURD_REGIME_FILE}" BURC_TABTARGET_DELIMITER 1 8
  zbud_check_string "${BURD_REGIME_FILE}" BURC_TEMP_ROOT_DIR       1 256
  zbud_check_string "${BURD_REGIME_FILE}" BURC_OUTPUT_ROOT_DIR     1 256
  zbud_check_string "${BURD_REGIME_FILE}" BURC_TOOLS_DIR           1 256

  # Dispatch-provided directory variables (survive exec boundary for CLIs)
  BURD_TOOLS_DIR="${BURC_TOOLS_DIR}"
  BURD_BUK_DIR="${BURC_TOOLS_DIR}/buk"
  BURD_TABTARGET_DIR="${BURC_TABTARGET_DIR}"
  export BURD_TOOLS_DIR BURD_BUK_DIR BURD_TABTARGET_DIR

  # Source station file (skip for no-log handbook tabtargets)
  if test -z "${BURD_NO_LOG:-}"; then
    zbud_show "Sourcing station file: ${BURC_STATION_FILE}"
    source                           "${BURC_STATION_FILE}"

    # Validate station variables
    zbud_check_string "${BURC_STATION_FILE}" BURS_LOG_DIR 1 256
  fi

  mkdir -p "${BURC_TEMP_ROOT_DIR}" || zbud_die "Failed to create temp root: ${BURC_TEMP_ROOT_DIR}"
  local -r z_date_file="${BURC_TEMP_ROOT_DIR}/bud_bootstrap_date.txt"
  date +'%Y%m%d-%H%M%S %s' > "${z_date_file}" || zbud_die "Failed to get datetime"
  local z_datetime
  z_datetime=$(<"${z_date_file}")
  test -n "${z_datetime}" || zbud_die "Empty datetime from ${z_date_file}"
  BURD_NOW_STAMP="${z_datetime% *}-$$-$((RANDOM % 1000))"
  BURD_NOW_EPOCH="${z_datetime#* }"
  zbud_show "Generated timestamp: ${BURD_NOW_STAMP} epoch: ${BURD_NOW_EPOCH}"

  BURD_TEMP_DIR="${BURC_TEMP_ROOT_DIR}/temp-${BURD_NOW_STAMP}"
  case "${BURD_TEMP_DIR}" in
    /*) ;;
    *)  BURD_TEMP_DIR="${PWD}/${BURD_TEMP_DIR}" ;;
  esac
  mkdir -p                           "${BURD_TEMP_DIR}" || zbud_die "Failed to create temp directory: ${BURD_TEMP_DIR}"
  zbud_show "Generated temporary dir: ${BURD_TEMP_DIR}"

  # Setup transcript file path
  BURD_TRANSCRIPT="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/transcript.txt"

  # Setup output directory (fixed location, cleared on each run)
  BURD_OUTPUT_DIR="${BURC_OUTPUT_ROOT_DIR}/current"
  case "${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}" in
    /*) ;;
    *)  BURD_OUTPUT_DIR="${PWD}/${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}" ;;
  esac

  # Clear if exists, then create fresh
  if test -d "${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}"; then
    zbud_show "Clearing existing output directory: ${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}"
    rm -rf "${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}" || zbud_die "Failed to remove output directory: ${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}"
  fi
  mkdir -p "${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}" || zbud_die "Failed to create output directory: ${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}"

  zbud_show "Output directory ready: ${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}"

  # Get Git context
  local -r z_git_context_file="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bud_git_context.txt"
  local -r z_git_context_stderr="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/bud_git_context_stderr.txt"
  if git describe --always --dirty --tags --long > "${z_git_context_file}" 2>"${z_git_context_stderr}"; then
    BURD_GIT_CONTEXT=$(<"${z_git_context_file}")
  else
    BURD_GIT_CONTEXT="git-unavailable"
  fi
  zbud_show "Git context: ${BURD_GIT_CONTEXT}"

  # Export for child processes
  export BURD_TEMP_DIR
  export BURD_OUTPUT_DIR
  export BURD_NOW_STAMP
  export BURD_NOW_EPOCH
  export BURD_TRANSCRIPT
  export BURD_GIT_CONTEXT

  return 0
}

# Process command-line arguments
zbud_process_args() {
  local -r z_target="${1}"
  shift

  zbud_show "Processing target: ${z_target}"

content.text:
Acknowledged on logging — no redirect.

### File precision

Both list and loop live in `Tools/rbk/rbq_Qualify.sh` (the existing module). No new file.

- **List**: as a kindle constant in `zrbq_kindle()`, declared after `zrbz_sentinel` so `RBZ_*` constants are in scope.
- **Loop**: new function `rbq_qualify_handbooks()` as a sibling to `rbq_qualify_colophons` / `rbq_qualify_context` / `rbq_qualify_fast` / `rbq_qualify_release`.

### buz utilities availability — yes, fully

`rbq_cli.sh:36-37` sources `buz_zipper.sh` + `rbz_zipper.sh`; `rbq_cli.sh:42-43` calls `zbuz_kindle` + `zrbz_kindle` *before* `zrbq_kindle`. By the time `rbq_*` functions run, all `RBZ_*` constants and `z_buz_*` registry arrays are in scope.

Existing `rbq_qualify_colophons` already proves this — it iterates `z_buz_colophon_roll` and reads `z_buz_module_roll` directly (lines 95-114 of `rbq_Qualify.sh`).

### Concrete loop

```bash
rbq_qualify_handbooks() {
  zrbq_sentinel

  buc_step "Qualifying handbook displays"

  local -r z_workbench="${RBCC_KIT_DIR}/rbw_workbench.sh"
  local z_pass=0
  local z_fail=()
  local z_colophon=""

  for z_colophon in "${ZRBQ_HANDBOOK_COLOPHONS[@]}"; do
    buc_step "Handbook: ${z_colophon}"
    if "${z_workbench}" "${z_colophon}"; then
      z_pass=$((z_pass + 1))
    else
      z_fail+=("${z_colophon}")
    fi
  done

  local -r z_total="${#ZRBQ_HANDBOOK_COLOPHONS[@]}"
  buc_log_args "Handbook qualification: ${z_pass}/${z_total} passed"

  if (( ${#z_fail[@]} )); then
    local z_f=""
    for z_f in "${z_fail[@]}"; do
      buc_warn "  ✗ ${z_f}"
    done
    buc_die "Handbook qualification failed: ${#z_fail[@]} of ${z_total}"
  fi
}
```

Each subprocess inherits the qualifier's `BURD_*` environment, including `BURD_LOG_*`. Handbook output streams to the qualifier's stdout/stderr — which BUK logging captures into the qualifier's own hist log. One log entry covers the whole sweep with each handbook's output inline. If a handbook subprocess dies, its exit propagates and the qualifier records the failure.

`RBCC_KIT_DIR` is from `rbcc_Constants.sh` (kindled by `zrbcc_kindle` which `zrbq_kindle` already sentinels). `rbw_workbench.sh:rbw_main` accepts the colophon as its first arg and routes via `rbw_route` → `buz_exec_lookup`.

### Total change set

1. `Tools/rbk/rbq_Qualify.sh` — add `ZRBQ_HANDBOOK_COLOPHONS` to `zrbq_kindle`; add `rbq_qualify_handbooks` function.
2. `Tools/rbk/rbz_zipper.sh` — enroll `RBZ_QUALIFY_HANDBOOKS "rbw-th" "rbq_cli.sh" "rbq_qualify_handbooks" "" "..."` in Theurge group.
3. `tt/rbw-th.HandbookQualify.sh` — new launcher (standard pattern).
4. `Tools/rbk/rbk-claude-tabtarget-context.md` — regenerate via `tt/rbw-MG.MarshalGenerate.sh` after enrollment.

Ready?

Lets be clear: I do _not_ want bleed through from the environment where qualify is running into the tabtarget invocation: that changes the way that the routines work.  Study the test approach to see how we can run each handbook in 'vanilla' mode, as much as possible.

100	
101	buto_success() {
102	  echo "${ZBUTO_GREEN}PASSED:${ZBUTO_RESET} $*" >&2
103	}
104	
105	######################################################################
106	# BURV bridge support
107	
108	# zbuto_next_invoke_capture() - Find next unused invoke number
109	# Scans BUTE_BURV_ROOT/invoke-NNNNN directories for next available slot
110	# Returns: next invoke number (starts at 10000)
111	zbuto_next_invoke_capture() {
112	  local z_burv_root="${BUTE_BURV_ROOT:-}"
113	  test -n "${z_burv_root}" || return 1
114	
115	  local z_invoke_num=10000
116	  while test -d "${z_burv_root}/invoke-${z_invoke_num}"; do
117	    z_invoke_num=$((z_invoke_num + 1))
118	  done
119	  echo "${z_invoke_num}"
120	}
121	
122	######################################################################
123	# Safely invoke a command under 'set -e', capturing stdout, stderr, and exit status
124	# Globals set:
125	#   ZBUTO_STDOUT       - command stdout
126	#   ZBUTO_STDERR       - command stderr
127	#   ZBUTO_STATUS       - command exit code
128	#   ZBUTO_BURV_OUTPUT      - BURV output root (empty if BURV not enabled)
129	#   ZBUTO_BURV_OUTPUT_DIR  - BURV output dir matching BURD_OUTPUT_DIR (empty if BURV not enabled)
130	# BURV bridge: If BUTE_BURV_ROOT is set, creates per-invocation BURV isolation
131	
132	zbuto_invoke() {
133	  buto_trace "Invoking: $*"
134	
135	  local z_tmp_stdout
136	  z_tmp_stdout=$(mktemp)
137	  local z_tmp_stderr
138	  z_tmp_stderr=$(mktemp)
139	
140	  # BURV bridge setup if enabled
141	  local z_burv_output=""
142	  local z_burv_temp=""
143	  if test -n "${BUTE_BURV_ROOT:-}"; then
144	    local z_invoke_num
145	    z_invoke_num=$(zbuto_next_invoke_capture)
146	    local z_invoke_dir="${BUTE_BURV_ROOT}/invoke-${z_invoke_num}"
147	    z_burv_output="${z_invoke_dir}/output"
148	    z_burv_temp="${z_invoke_dir}/temp"
149	    mkdir -p "${z_burv_output}" "${z_burv_temp}"
150	  fi
151	
152	  ZBUTO_STATUS=$( (
153	      set +e
154	      if test -n "${z_burv_output}"; then
155	        BURV_OUTPUT_ROOT_DIR="${z_burv_output}" \
156	        BURV_TEMP_ROOT_DIR="${z_burv_temp}" \
157	          "$@" >"${z_tmp_stdout}" 2>"${z_tmp_stderr}"
158	      else
159	        "$@" >"${z_tmp_stdout}" 2>"${z_tmp_stderr}"
160	      fi
161	      printf '%s' "$?"
162	      exit 0

buto_success() {
  echo "${ZBUTO_GREEN}PASSED:${ZBUTO_RESET} $*" >&2
}

######################################################################
# BURV bridge support

# zbuto_next_invoke_capture() - Find next unused invoke number
# Scans BUTE_BURV_ROOT/invoke-NNNNN directories for next available slot
# Returns: next invoke number (starts at 10000)
zbuto_next_invoke_capture() {
  local z_burv_root="${BUTE_BURV_ROOT:-}"
  test -n "${z_burv_root}" || return 1

  local z_invoke_num=10000
  while test -d "${z_burv_root}/invoke-${z_invoke_num}"; do
    z_invoke_num=$((z_invoke_num + 1))
  done
  echo "${z_invoke_num}"
}

######################################################################
# Safely invoke a command under 'set -e', capturing stdout, stderr, and exit status
# Globals set:
#   ZBUTO_STDOUT       - command stdout
#   ZBUTO_STDERR       - command stderr
#   ZBUTO_STATUS       - command exit code
#   ZBUTO_BURV_OUTPUT      - BURV output root (empty if BURV not enabled)
#   ZBUTO_BURV_OUTPUT_DIR  - BURV output dir matching BURD_OUTPUT_DIR (empty if BURV not enabled)
# BURV bridge: If BUTE_BURV_ROOT is set, creates per-invocation BURV isolation

zbuto_invoke() {
  buto_trace "Invoking: $*"

  local z_tmp_stdout
  z_tmp_stdout=$(mktemp)
  local z_tmp_stderr
  z_tmp_stderr=$(mktemp)

  # BURV bridge setup if enabled
  local z_burv_output=""
  local z_burv_temp=""
  if test -n "${BUTE_BURV_ROOT:-}"; then
    local z_invoke_num
    z_invoke_num=$(zbuto_next_invoke_capture)
    local z_invoke_dir="${BUTE_BURV_ROOT}/invoke-${z_invoke_num}"
    z_burv_output="${z_invoke_dir}/output"
    z_burv_temp="${z_invoke_dir}/temp"
    mkdir -p "${z_burv_output}" "${z_burv_temp}"
  fi

  ZBUTO_STATUS=$( (
      set +e
      if test -n "${z_burv_output}"; then
        BURV_OUTPUT_ROOT_DIR="${z_burv_output}" \
        BURV_TEMP_ROOT_DIR="${z_burv_temp}" \
          "$@" >"${z_tmp_stdout}" 2>"${z_tmp_stderr}"
      else
        "$@" >"${z_tmp_stdout}" 2>"${z_tmp_stderr}"
      fi
      printf '%s' "$?"
      exit 0
    ) || printf '__subshell_failed__' )

  if test "${ZBUTO_STATUS}" = "__subshell_failed__" || test -z "${ZBUTO_STATUS}"; then
    ZBUTO_STATUS=127
    ZBUTO_STDOUT=""
    ZBUTO_STDERR="zbuto_invoke: command caused shell to exit before status could be captured"
  else
    ZBUTO_STDOUT=$(<"${z_tmp_stdout}")
    ZBUTO_STDERR=$(<"${z_tmp_stderr}")
  fi

  rm -f "${z_tmp_stdout}" "${z_tmp_stderr}"

  ZBUTO_BURV_OUTPUT="${z_burv_output}"
  ZBUTO_BURV_OUTPUT_DIR="${z_burv_output:+${z_burv_output}/current}"
}

######################################################################
# buto_unit_* - Raw command invocation via zbuto_invoke

buto_unit_expect_ok_stdout() {
  set -e

  local z_expected="${1}"; shift

  zbuto_invoke "$@"

  buto_fatal_on_error "${ZBUTO_STATUS}" "Command failed with status ${ZBUTO_STATUS}" \
                                        "Command: $*"                               \
                                        "STDERR: ${ZBUTO_STDERR}"

  test "${ZBUTO_STDOUT}" = "${z_expected}" || buto_fatal "Output mismatch"            \
                                                         "Command: $*"                \
                                                         "Expected: '${z_expected}'"  \
                                                         "Got:      '${ZBUTO_STDOUT}'"
}

buto_unit_expect_ok() {
  set -e

  zbuto_invoke "$@"

  buto_fatal_on_error "${ZBUTO_STATUS}" "Command failed with status ${ZBUTO_STATUS}" \
                                        "Command: $*"                               \
                                        "STDERR: ${ZBUTO_STDERR}"
}

buto_unit_expect_fatal() {
  set -e

  zbuto_invoke "$@"

  buto_fatal_on_success "${ZBUTO_STATUS}" "Expected failure but got success" \
                                          "Command: $*"                      \
                                          "STDOUT: ${ZBUTO_STDOUT}"          \
                                          "STDERR: ${ZBUTO_STDERR}"
}

######################################################################
# buto_tt_* - Tabtarget file invocation (requires tabtarget exists)
#
# Resolves colophon to tt/{colophon}.*.sh file, dies if missing.
# Extra args pass through to tabtarget script.

zbuto_resolve_tabtarget() {
  local z_colophon="${1:-}"
  test -n "${z_colophon}" || buto_fatal "zbuto_resolve_tabtarget: colophon required"

  local z_tt_dir="${BURC_TABTARGET_DIR:-}"
  test -n "${z_tt_dir}" || buto_fatal "BURC_TABTARGET_DIR not set -- buto_tt requires BUK environment"
  local z_matches=("${z_tt_dir}/${z_colophon}."*.sh)

  # Bash 3.2: no-match glob returns literal — check with test -e
  test -e "${z_matches[0]}" || buto_fatal "No tabtarget found for colophon '${z_colophon}' in ${z_tt_dir}/"

  test "${#z_matches[@]}" -eq 1 || buto_fatal "Multiple tabtargets found for colophon '${z_colophon}' in ${z_tt_dir}/"

  echo "${z_matches[0]}"
}

buto_tt_expect_ok() {
  set -e

  local z_colophon="${1:-}"
  test -n "${z_colophon}" || buto_fatal "buto_tt_expect_ok: colophon required"
  shift

  local z_tabtarget
  z_tabtarget=$(zbuto_resolve_tabtarget "${z_colophon}")

  zbuto_invoke "${z_tabtarget}" "$@"

  buto_fatal_on_error "${ZBUTO_STATUS}" "Tabtarget failed with status ${ZBUTO_STATUS}" \
                                        "Colophon: ${z_colophon}"                      \
                                        "Tabtarget: ${z_tabtarget}"                    \
                                        "STDERR: ${ZBUTO_STDERR}"
}

buto_tt_previous_output_capture() {
  test -n "${ZBUTO_BURV_OUTPUT_DIR:-}" || buto_fatal "No previous tabtarget output"
  echo "${ZBUTO_BURV_OUTPUT_DIR}"
}

buto_tt_expect_fatal() {
  set -e

  local z_colophon="${1:-}"
  test -n "${z_colophon}" || buto_fatal "buto_tt_expect_fatal: colophon required"
  shift

  local z_tabtarget
  z_tabtarget=$(zbuto_resolve_tabtarget "${z_colophon}")

  zbuto_invoke "${z_tabtarget}" "$@"

  buto_fatal_on_success "${ZBUTO_STATUS}" "Expected failure but got success"    \
                                          "Colophon: ${z_colophon}"             \
                                          "Tabtarget: ${z_tabtarget}"           \
                                          "STDOUT: ${ZBUTO_STDOUT}"             \
                                          "STDERR: ${ZBUTO_STDERR}"
}

######################################################################
# buto_launch_* - Workbench dispatch (no tabtarget file required)
#
# First arg is launcher (workbench), second is colophon, rest are args.
# Invokes launcher directly with colophon+args.

buto_launch_expect_ok() {
  set -e

  local z_launcher="${1:-}"
  local z_colophon="${2:-}"
  test -n "${z_launcher}" || buto_fatal "buto_launch_expect_ok: launcher required"
  test -n "${z_colophon}" || buto_fatal "buto_launch_expect_ok: colophon required"
  shift 2

  zbuto_invoke "${z_launcher}" "${z_colophon}" "$@"

  buto_fatal_on_error "${ZBUTO_STATUS}" "Launch failed with status ${ZBUTO_STATUS}" \
                                        "Launcher: ${z_launcher}"                   \
                                        "Colophon: ${z_colophon}"                   \
                                        "STDERR: ${ZBUTO_STDERR}"
}

buto_launch_expect_fatal() {
  set -e

  local z_launcher="${1:-}"
  local z_colophon="${2:-}"
  test -n "${z_launcher}" || buto_fatal "buto_launch_expect_fatal: launcher required"
  test -n "${z_colophon}" || buto_fatal "buto_launch_expect_fatal: colophon required"
  shift 2

  zbuto_invoke "${z_launcher}" "${z_colophon}" "$@"

  buto_fatal_on_success "${ZBUTO_STATUS}" "Expected failure but got success" \
                                          "Launcher: ${z_launcher}"          \
                                          "Colophon: ${z_colophon}"          \
                                          "STDOUT: ${ZBUTO_STDOUT}"          \
                                          "STDERR: ${ZBUTO_STDERR}"
}

# eof

450	
451	Three tiers provide increasing levels of resolution:
452	
453	[[busit_unit]]
454	//axl_voices axo_entity
455	{busit_unit}::
456	Invokes a raw function or command directly via `zbuto_invoke`.
457	No tabtarget file or workbench routing involved.
458	+
459	API: `buto_unit_expect_ok(command...)`, `buto_unit_expect_fatal(command...)`, `buto_unit_expect_ok_stdout(expected, command...)`
460	
461	[[busit_tabtarget]]
462	//axl_voices axo_entity
463	{busit_tabtarget}::
464	Resolves a
465	{busdc_colophon}
466	to a
467	{busdl_launcher}
468	file in `tt/`, then invokes it via `zbuto_invoke`.
469	Dies if no tabtarget matches or if multiple matches exist.
470	+
471	API: `buto_tt_expect_ok(colophon, args...)`, `buto_tt_expect_fatal(colophon, args...)`
472	
473	[[busit_launch]]
474	//axl_voices axo_entity
475	{busit_launch}::
476	Invokes a workbench dispatch function directly, bypassing the
477	{busdl_launcher}
478	file.
479	Useful when exercising workbench routing without a tabtarget on disk.
480	+
481	API: `buto_launch_expect_ok(launcher, colophon, args...)`, `buto_launch_expect_fatal(launcher, colophon, args...)`
482	
483	=== Capture Globals
484	
485	Every call to `zbuto_invoke` sets four globals that subsequent assertions can inspect.
486	
487	[[busig_stdout]]
488	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string
489	{busig_stdout}::
490	Captured stdout from the invoked command.
491	
492	[[busig_stderr]]
493	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string
494	{busig_stderr}::
495	Captured stderr from the invoked command.
496	
497	[[busig_status]]
498	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_integer
499	{busig_status}::
500	Exit code from the invoked command.
501	
502	[[busig_burv_output]]
503	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path
504	{busig_burv_output}::
505	BURV output directory for the invocation.
506	Empty when
507	{busx_burv_root}
508	is not set.
509	When set, contains the invocation's isolated output tree rooted at
510	`{BUTE_BURV_ROOT}/invoke-NNNNN/output`.
511	
512	=== Case Boundary
513	
514	[[busx_tcase]]
515	//axl_voices axo_entity
516	{busx_tcase}::
517	The case isolation runner (`zbute_tcase`).
518	Executes a single test case function in a subshell with per-case temporary state.
519	Sets
520	{busx_temp_dir}
521	and
522	{busx_burv_root}
523	before invoking the case function.
524	+
525	The subshell boundary ensures that variable mutations, function redefinitions, and side effects in one case cannot leak to another.
526	
527	[[busx_temp_dir]]
528	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path
529	{busx_temp_dir}::
530	Per-case temporary directory (`BUT_TEMP_DIR`), set by
531	{busx_tcase}.
532	Created as a subdirectory of the fixture's root temp dir, named after the case function.
533	
534	[[busx_burv_root]]
535	//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path
536	{busx_burv_root}::
537	Per-case BURV isolation root (`BUTE_BURV_ROOT`), set by
538	{busx_tcase}.
539	When present, all invocation tiers create per-invoke BURV directories under this root, enabling multi-step test sequences to produce and consume isolated output.
540	
541	=== Fact-File Pattern
542	
543	[[busff_fact_file]]
544	//axl_voices axo_entity
545	{busff_fact_file}::
546	Inter-step communication pattern using named output files.
547	+
548	A producer writes a value to `${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/${CONSTANT}` during command execution.
549	A consumer reads it from `${ZBUTO_BURV_OUTPUT}/current/${CONSTANT}` after invocation.
550	The kindle constant name is the contract between producer and consumer.
551	+
552	Example:
553	+
554	[source,bash]
555	----
556	# Producer (in command implementation):
557	echo "${z_hallmark}" > "${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/${RBF_FACT_HALLMARK}"
558	
559	# Consumer (in test case, after buto_tt_expect_ok):
560	z_hallmark=$(<"${ZBUTO_BURV_OUTPUT}/current/${RBF_FACT_HALLMARK}")
561	----
562	+
563	The `current/` path segment maps
564	{burd_output_dir}
565	inside the BURV isolation tree.
566	Fact-file constants are defined as `readonly` in kindle (e.g., `RBF_FACT_HALLMARK`).
567	
568	[[buf_write_fact]]
569	//axl_voices axo_entity

Dies if no tabtarget matches or if multiple matches exist.
+
API: `buto_tt_expect_ok(colophon, args...)`, `buto_tt_expect_fatal(colophon, args...)`

[[busit_launch]]
//axl_voices axo_entity
{busit_launch}::
Invokes a workbench dispatch function directly, bypassing the
{busdl_launcher}
file.
Useful when exercising workbench routing without a tabtarget on disk.
+
API: `buto_launch_expect_ok(launcher, colophon, args...)`, `buto_launch_expect_fatal(launcher, colophon, args...)`

=== Capture Globals

Every call to `zbuto_invoke` sets four globals that subsequent assertions can inspect.

[[busig_stdout]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string
{busig_stdout}::
Captured stdout from the invoked command.

[[busig_stderr]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_string
{busig_stderr}::
Captured stderr from the invoked command.

[[busig_status]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_integer
{busig_status}::
Exit code from the invoked command.

[[busig_burv_output]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path
{busig_burv_output}::
BURV output directory for the invocation.
Empty when
{busx_burv_root}
is not set.
When set, contains the invocation's isolated output tree rooted at
`{BUTE_BURV_ROOT}/invoke-NNNNN/output`.

=== Case Boundary

[[busx_tcase]]
//axl_voices axo_entity
{busx_tcase}::
The case isolation runner (`zbute_tcase`).
Executes a single test case function in a subshell with per-case temporary state.
Sets
{busx_temp_dir}
and
{busx_burv_root}
before invoking the case function.
+
The subshell boundary ensures that variable mutations, function redefinitions, and side effects in one case cannot leak to another.

[[busx_temp_dir]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path
{busx_temp_dir}::
Per-case temporary directory (`BUT_TEMP_DIR`), set by
{busx_tcase}.
Created as a subdirectory of the fixture's root temp dir, named after the case function.

[[busx_burv_root]]
//axl_voices axrg_variable axtu_path
{busx_burv_root}::
Per-case BURV isolation root (`BUTE_BURV_ROOT`), set by
{busx_tcase}.
When present, all invocation tiers create per-invoke BURV directories under this root, enabling multi-step test sequences to produce and consume isolated output.

=== Fact-File Pattern

[[busff_fact_file]]
//axl_voices axo_entity
{busff_fact_file}::
Inter-step communication pattern using named output files.
+
A producer writes a value to `${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/${CONSTANT}` during command execution.
A consumer reads it from `${ZBUTO_BURV_OUTPUT}/current/${CONSTANT}` after invocation.
The kindle constant name is the contract between producer and consumer.
+
Example:
+
[source,bash]
----
# Producer (in command implementation):
echo "${z_hallmark}" > "${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/${RBF_FACT_HALLMARK}"

# Consumer (in test case, after buto_tt_expect_ok):
z_hallmark=$(<"${ZBUTO_BURV_OUTPUT}/current/${RBF_FACT_HALLMARK}")
----
+
The `current/` path segment maps
{burd_output_dir}
inside the BURV isolation tree.
Fact-file constants are defined as `readonly` in kindle (e.g., `RBF_FACT_HALLMARK`).

[[buf_write_fact]]
//axl_voices axo_entity

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at

#

# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# BUK Test Engine - _tcase boundary runner

set -euo pipefail

# Source test-case API
ZBUTE_SCRIPT_DIR="${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}"
source "${ZBUTE_SCRIPT_DIR}/buto_operations.sh"

######################################################################
# _tcase boundary — case isolation subshell

# _tcase boundary runner: execute case function in isolation subshell
zbute_tcase() {
  set -e

  local z_case_name="${1}"
  declare -F "${z_case_name}" >/dev/null || buto_fatal "Test function not found: ${z_case_name}"

  buto_section "START: ${z_case_name}"

  local z_case_temp_dir="${ZBUTE_ROOT_TEMP_DIR}/${z_case_name}"
  mkdir -p "${z_case_temp_dir}" || buto_fatal "Failed to create test temp dir: ${z_case_temp_dir}"

  local z_status=0
  (
    set -e
    export BUT_TEMP_DIR="${z_case_temp_dir}"
    export BUTE_BURV_ROOT="${z_case_temp_dir}/burv"
    "${z_case_name}"
  ) || z_status=$?

  buto_trace "Ran: ${z_case_name} and got status:${z_status}"
  buto_fatal_on_error "${z_status}" "Test failed: ${z_case_name}"

  buto_trace "Finished: ${z_case_name} with status: ${z_status}"
  echo "${ZBUTO_GREEN}PASSED:${ZBUTO_RESET} ${z_case_name}" >&2
}

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at

# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# BUTT Testbench - BUK test framework self-test
#
# Exercises the BUK test framework (bute/butr/butd/buto) with kick-tires
# and BURE tweak cases.  Pure local — no GCP, no containers, no network.

set -euo pipefail

BUTT_SCRIPT_DIR="${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}"
BUTT_BUTS_DIR="${BUTT_SCRIPT_DIR}/buts"

# Source dependencies
source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buc_command.sh"
source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/burd_regime.sh"
source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buv_validation.sh"
source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/bute_engine.sh"
source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/butr_registry.sh"
source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/butd_dispatch.sh"
source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/bure_regime.sh"
source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buf_fact.sh"
source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buym_yelp.sh"
source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buh_handbook.sh"

# Source test case files
source "${BUTT_BUTS_DIR}/butckk_KickTires.sh"
source "${BUTT_BUTS_DIR}/butcbe_BureEnvironment.sh"
source "${BUTT_BUTS_DIR}/butcbx_BurxExchange.sh"
source "${BUTT_BUTS_DIR}/butclc_LinkCombinator.sh"
source "${BUTT_BUTS_DIR}/butcym_YelpModule.sh"

buc_context "${0##*/}"
zbuv_kindle
zburd_kindle

######################################################################
# Registration

butt_kindle() {
  butr_kindle

  # All fixtures are pure local
  butr_suite_enroll "self-test"

  # kick-tires fixture (2 cases)
  butr_fixture_enroll "kick-tires" "" "zbutt_noop_baste"
  butr_case_enroll "kick-tires" butckk_false_tcase
  butr_case_enroll "kick-tires" butckk_true_tcase

  # bure-tweak fixture (9 cases)
  butr_fixture_enroll "bure-tweak" "" "zbutt_noop_baste"
  butr_case_enroll "bure-tweak" butcbe_tweak_empty_tcase
  butr_case_enroll "bure-tweak" butcbe_tweak_both_set_tcase
  butr_case_enroll "bure-tweak" butcbe_tweak_name_only_tcase
  butr_case_enroll "bure-tweak" butcbe_tweak_value_only_tcase
  butr_case_enroll "bure-tweak" butcbe_tweak_name_too_long_tcase
  butr_case_enroll "bure-tweak" butcbe_tweak_value_too_long_tcase
  butr_case_enroll "bure-tweak" butcbe_label_valid_tcase
  butr_case_enroll "bure-tweak" butcbe_label_too_long_tcase
  butr_case_enroll "bure-tweak" butcbe_unexpected_var_tcase

  # burx-exchange fixture (4 cases)
  butr_fixture_enroll "burx-exchange" "" "zbutt_noop_baste"
  butr_case_enroll "burx-exchange" butcbx_burx_dual_write_tcase
  butr_case_enroll "burx-exchange" butcbx_burx_fields_tcase
  butr_case_enroll "burx-exchange" butcbx_burx_preexist_tcase
  butr_case_enroll "burx-exchange" butcbx_burx_timestamp_format_tcase

  # buh-link fixture (3 cases)
  butr_fixture_enroll "buh-link" "" "zbutt_noop_baste"
  butr_case_enroll "buh-link" butclc_link_osc8_tcase
  butr_case_enroll "buh-link" butclc_link_fallback_tcase
  butr_case_enroll "buh-link" butclc_link_variants_tcase

  # buym-yelp fixture (7 cases)
  butr_fixture_enroll "buym-yelp" "" "zbutt_noop_baste"
  butr_case_enroll "buym-yelp" butcym_cmd_resolve_tcase
  butr_case_enroll "buym-yelp" butcym_link_osc8_tcase
  butr_case_enroll "buym-yelp" butcym_link_fallback_tcase
  butr_case_enroll "buym-yelp" butcym_ambient_preservation_tcase
  butr_case_enroll "buym-yelp" butcym_fast_path_tcase
  butr_case_enroll "buym-yelp" butcym_multi_markers_tcase
  butr_case_enroll "buym-yelp" butcym_plain_mode_tcase
}

zbutt_noop_baste() {
  buto_trace "Baste (no-op)"
}

######################################################################
# Routing

butt_route() {
  local -r z_command="${1:-}"
  shift || true

  zburd_sentinel

  butt_kindle

  export ZBUTE_ROOT_TEMP_DIR="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}"
  export BUT_VERBOSE="${BUT_VERBOSE:-0}"

  case "${z_command}" in
    buw-st)
      butd_run_suite "self-test"
      ;;
    *)
      buc_die "Unknown command: ${z_command}"
      ;;
  esac
}

butt_main() {
  local -r z_command="${1:-}"
  shift || true
  test -n "${z_command}" || buc_die "No command specified"
  butt_route "${z_command}" "$@"
}

butt_main "$@"

# eof

content.text:
Found the BUK test isolation machinery. Here's what's there.

### Three invocation tiers (`Tools/buk/buto_operations.sh`)

| Helper | What it invokes | Internally globs? |
|---|---|---|
| `buto_unit_*` | Raw command/function direct call | No |
| `buto_tt_*` | Tabtarget file (resolves colophon→file) | **Yes** (line 233; asserts exactly one match, dies if not) |
| `buto_launch_*` | Workbench dispatch by colophon (bypasses tabtarget file) | No |

All three route through one primitive: **`zbuto_invoke`** (lines 132-178). It runs the command in a subshell, captures stdout/stderr/status to mktemp files. Doesn't die on failure — sets `ZBUTO_STATUS`.

### The isolation primitive: BURV bridge

When `BUTE_BURV_ROOT` is set, `zbuto_invoke`:
1. Picks next invoke number, creates `${BUTE_BURV_ROOT}/invoke-NNNNN/{output,temp}/`
2. Exports `BURV_OUTPUT_ROOT_DIR=...` and `BURV_TEMP_ROOT_DIR=...` for the subprocess

Then `bud_dispatch.sh:65-66` reads those `BURV_*` env vars and **overrides** `BURC_OUTPUT_ROOT_DIR` / `BURC_TEMP_ROOT_DIR`. Result: each handbook subprocess gets its own fresh `BURD_OUTPUT_DIR` and `BURD_TEMP_DIR` — qualifier's dirs untouched.

This is exactly the bleed-through prevention you wanted, **but it only engages if the subprocess actually runs `bud_dispatch.sh`**. Workbench-direct invocation skips dispatch and BURV does nothing.

### What "vanilla" actually gets you

| Aspect | `buto_tt_*` (via tabtarget) | `buto_launch_*` (via workbench) |
|---|---|---|
| Goes through bud_dispatch | yes — full chain | no — workbench direct |
| BURV bridge engages | yes | no |
| Fresh `BURD_OUTPUT_DIR`, `BURD_TEMP_DIR` | yes | inherited |
| Fresh `BURD_NOW_STAMP`, `BURD_GIT_CONTEXT` | yes (dispatch regenerates) | inherited |
| Fresh `BURD_LOG_*` | yes (dispatch regenerates) | inherited |
| Fresh `BURD_TOKEN_*` | yes (parsed from filename) | inherited |
| Resolves colophon by globbing | **yes** (asserted single match) | no — workbench dispatches via registry |

So the choice is sharp: **glob-with-assertion gets full vanilla; no-glob gets only partial isolation**. There's no middle ground in the existing helpers.

### Third option (custom, not in BUK)

Invoke `bud_dispatch.sh` directly with a synthetic tabtarget filename:

```bash
"${BURD_BUK_DIR}/bud_dispatch.sh" "${z_colophon}.QualifySweep.sh"
```

`bud_dispatch.sh` parses the filename for tokens but **never tests file existence** (verified — lines 150-199). It would tokenize, set up fresh `BURD_*`, exec the workbench coordinator. With `BURV_OUTPUT_ROOT_DIR`/`BURV_TEMP_ROOT_DIR` exported beforehand, BURV bridge engages. Synthetic frontispiece "QualifySweep" appears in `BURD_LOG_HIST` filenames — clear audit trail.

This gets full vanilla AND no globbing AND uses only registry-known colophons. It's slightly off the documented test pattern (no buto helper for it), but mechanically clean.

### Recommendation

Use the third option, with `zbuto_invoke` for status capture and BURV setup:

```bash
rbq_qualify_handbooks() {
  zrbq_sentinel

  buc_step "Qualifying handbook displays"

  local -r z_dispatch="${BURD_BUK_DIR}/bud_dispatch.sh"
  local -r z_burv_root="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbq_handbook_burv"
  mkdir -p "${z_burv_root}"
  export BUTE_BURV_ROOT="${z_burv_root}"

  local z_pass=0
  local z_fail=()
  local z_colophon=""

  for z_colophon in "${ZRBQ_HANDBOOK_COLOPHONS[@]}"; do
    buc_step "Handbook: ${z_colophon}"
    zbuto_invoke "${z_dispatch}" "${z_colophon}.QualifySweep.sh"
    if test "${ZBUTO_STATUS}" = "0"; then
      z_pass=$((z_pass + 1))
    else
      z_fail+=("${z_colophon}")
    fi
  done

  ...
}
```

Furnish adds: `source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buto_operations.sh"` (the buto API; `but_test.sh` is a compat shim).

### Caveat

The buto helpers (`buto_*_expect_ok`, etc.) call `buto_fatal` on failure — exits the qualifier. Using `zbuto_invoke` directly is the underlying primitive that *captures* status without dying, which is what we need for fail-soft sweep. It's `z*` prefixed (internal-by-convention), but it's the right tool. Alternative: use the public helpers in subshells with `||` to catch.

Want me to proceed with this shape — `zbuto_invoke` + synthetic tabtarget filename through `bud_dispatch.sh` for full vanilla isolation?

content.text:
Looked at the test infrastructure layout. Two natural homes:

### Option A — fixture in existing Theurge fast suite

Theurge (`Tools/rbk/rbtd/`) is the Rust test orchestrator. Its `rbtdrf_fast.rs` already hosts fast-tier fixtures (`enrollment-validation`, `regime-validation`, `regime-smoke`) — all of which shell out to bash and assert on exit codes. New fixture `handbook-render` would slot here as a peer:

```rust
// In rbtdrf_fast.rs (or a new rbtdrf_handbook.rs)
const HANDBOOK_COLOPHONS: &[&str] = &[
    "rbw-o", "rbw-Occ", "rbw-Ocr", "rbw-Ocd",
    "rbw-Ofc", "rbw-Odf", "rbw-Op", "rbw-Og",
    "rbw-h0", "rbw-hw", "rbw-HWdd", "rbw-HWdw", "rbw-HWdc",
    "rbw-gPE", "rbw-gPR", "rbw-gq",
];
// One case per colophon: invoke the tabtarget, assert exit 0
```

Each case runs in Theurge's existing isolation; failures are per-case (suite continues); all 16 results in one report. Wires into `tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh` automatically.

Cost: light Rust addition, ~1 file or extension to existing fast file.

### Option B — own Theurge suite

New fixture-set in its own suite (e.g., `display`), not bundled with fast/service/crucible/complete. Justification would be "even lighter than fast — zero deps, no BUV, just renders." But adds a new tabtarget tier (`tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.display.sh`) and conceptual surface.

### Option C — BUK's `butt_testbench.sh`

It already crosses into RBK with `butcrg_RegimeCredentials.sh` (uses `buto_tt_expect_ok "rbw-rav"`). Could add `butch_HandbookRender.sh` as a fixture file. Pure bash, no Rust touch. But it's BUK's self-test — bringing rbk handbook tests in stretches its scope.

### My read

**Option A**. Handbook coverage belongs in fast tier (no deps, runs everywhere) and Theurge already owns the orchestration vocabulary for it. The colophon list lives in Rust as a `&[&str]`, which makes it explicit and statically checked at compile time. Cases shell out to the actual tabtargets through Theurge's existing bash invocation helper — full BURV-style isolation comes for free since each invocation is its own subprocess with its own CWD/env.

The list is still hand-maintained — same discipline as before, just expressed in Rust syntax.

Caveat: I haven't read all of `rbtdri_invocation.rs` to know exactly what `rbtdri_find_tabtarget_global` does — name suggests it does globbing too. We'd want to confirm whether Theurge offers a "no-glob" colophon-to-invocation path, or if we accept the same "asserted single match" pattern that BUK's `buto_tt_*` uses.

### Pace direction

This shifts the work substantially:
- ₢A6AAn original docket (small bash bug fix) → not what gets done in this pace
- New work: add Rust fixture to Theurge, enumerate handbook colophons, run baseline, observe failures
- The original BCG windows crash gets demoted to "one of the failures the new fixture will reveal"

Should I:
1. Reslate ₢A6AAn with new docket reflecting the Theurge fixture approach + baseline + repair?
2. Or wrap ₢A6AAn (without the work) and enroll a fresh pace that's scoped to "handbook-render fixture + baseline run"?

The latter keeps history cleaner — ₢A6AAn was rough, this conversation reshaped the actual work into something else.

Your call on placement (A/B/C) and pace handling.

#
# Sourced by rbtw_workbench.sh. Provides rbte_dispatch() for command routing.
# Light furnish: buv + burd + buz + rbz + rbte (all commands)
# Heavy furnish: regime + constants + OAuth + IAM + Payor (probe only)

set -euo pipefail

ZRBTE_CLI_DIR="${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}"

######################################################################
# Light furnish — source and kindle for all commands

source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buv_validation.sh"
source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/burd_regime.sh"
source "${BURD_BUK_DIR}/buz_zipper.sh"
source "${ZRBTE_CLI_DIR}/../rbz_zipper.sh"
source "${ZRBTE_CLI_DIR}/rbte_engine.sh"

zbuv_kindle
zburd_kindle
zbuz_kindle
zrbz_kindle
zrbte_kindle

######################################################################
# Heavy furnish — regime + full module chain for access probe

zrbte_furnish_probe() {
  zburd_sentinel

  local z_rbk="${ZRBTE_CLI_DIR}/.."
  source "${z_rbk}/rbrr_regime.sh"
  source "${z_rbk}/rbrp_regime.sh"
  source "${z_rbk}/rbcc_Constants.sh"
  source "${z_rbk}/rbgc_Constants.sh"
  source "${z_rbk}/rbdc_DerivedConstants.sh"
  source "${z_rbk}/rbgo_OAuth.sh"
  source "${z_rbk}/rbgu_Utility.sh"
  source "${z_rbk}/rbgi_IAM.sh"
  source "${z_rbk}/rbgp_Payor.sh"
  source "${z_rbk}/rbgv_AccessProbe.sh"

  source "${RBBC_rbrr_file}" || buc_die "Failed to source ${RBBC_rbrr_file}"
  zrbrr_kindle
  zrbrr_enforce
  zrbcc_kindle
  zrbdc_kindle
  zrbgc_kindle
  zrbgo_kindle
  zrbgu_kindle
  zrbgi_kindle
  zrbgp_kindle
  zrbgv_kindle
}

######################################################################
# Dispatch

rbte_dispatch() {
  local z_command="$1"
  shift

  zburd_sentinel
  zrbte_sentinel

  case "${z_command}" in

    rbtd-b)
      rbte_build "$@"
      ;;

    rbtd-t)
      rbte_test "$@"
      ;;

    rbtd-r)
      rbte_run "$@"
      ;;

    rbtd-s)
      local z_frontispiece="${BURD_TOKEN_2:-}"
      case "${z_frontispiece}" in
        TestSuite)   rbte_suite "$@" ;;
        SingleCase)  rbte_single "$@" ;;
        *)           buc_die "Unknown frontispiece for rbtd-s: ${z_frontispiece} (expected TestSuite|SingleCase)" ;;
      esac
      ;;

    rbtd-ap)
      zrbte_furnish_probe
      rbte_probe "$@"
      ;;

    *)
      buc_die "Unknown command: ${z_command}"
      ;;

  esac
}

file.content:
test "${ZRBTE_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbte not kindled - call zrbte_kindle first"
}

######################################################################
# Internal helpers

zrbte_build_binary() {
  zrbte_sentinel

  buc_step "Building theurge"
  cargo build --manifest-path "${RBTE_MANIFEST}" || buc_die "cargo build failed"
  test -x "${ZRBTE_BINARY}" || buc_die "Theurge binary not found: ${ZRBTE_BINARY}"
}

zrbte_resolve_suite() {
  local z_suite="$1"
  case "${z_suite}" in
    fast)     echo "${ZRBTE_SUITE_FAST[*]}" ;;
    service)  echo "${ZRBTE_SUITE_SERVICE[*]}" ;;
    crucible) echo "${ZRBTE_SUITE_CRUCIBLE[*]}" ;;
    complete) echo "${ZRBTE_SUITE_COMPLETE[*]}" ;;
    *)        buc_die "Unknown suite: ${z_suite} (expected fast|service|crucible|complete)" ;;
  esac
}

######################################################################
# Public functions

rbte_build() {
  zrbte_sentinel

  buc_step "Building theurge"
  buc_log_args "Manifest: ${RBTE_MANIFEST}"
  cargo build --manifest-path "${RBTE_MANIFEST}" "$@" || buc_die "cargo build failed"
  buc_success "Theurge built"
}

rbte_test() {
  zrbte_sentinel

  buc_step "Testing theurge"
  buc_log_args "Manifest: ${RBTE_MANIFEST}"
  cargo test --manifest-path "${RBTE_MANIFEST}" "$@" || buc_die "cargo test failed"
  buc_success "All theurge tests passed"
}

rbte_run() {
  zrbte_sentinel

  local z_fixture="${BURD_TOKEN_3:-}"
  test -n "${z_fixture}" || buc_die "No fixture imprint — use tabtarget with imprint (e.g. rbtd-r.Run.tadmor.sh)"

  zrbte_build_binary

  buc_step "Running theurge fixture '${z_fixture}'"
  "${ZRBTE_BINARY}" "${ZRBTE_FULL_MANIFEST}" "${z_fixture}"
}

rbte_suite() {
  zrbte_sentinel

  local z_suite="${BURD_TOKEN_3:-}"
  test -n "${z_suite}" || buc_die "No suite imprint — use tabtarget with imprint (e.g. rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh)"

  local z_fixture_list
  z_fixture_list="$(zrbte_resolve_suite "${z_suite}")"

  zrbte_build_binary

  local z_count=0
  local z_fixture
  for z_fixture in ${z_fixture_list}; do
    buc_step "Running theurge fixture '${z_fixture}'"
    "${ZRBTE_BINARY}" "${ZRBTE_FULL_MANIFEST}" "${z_fixture}"
    z_count=$((z_count + 1))
  done

  buc_success "Suite '${z_suite}' complete (${z_count} fixtures)"
}

rbte_single() {
  zrbte_sentinel

  local z_fixture="${BURD_TOKEN_3:-}"
  test -n "${z_fixture}" || buc_die "No fixture imprint — use tabtarget with imprint (e.g. rbtd-s.SingleCase.tadmor.sh)"

  zrbte_build_binary

  local z_case="${1:-}"
  "${ZRBTE_BINARY}" single "${ZRBTE_FULL_MANIFEST}" "${z_fixture}" ${z_case:+"${z_case}"}
}

rbte_probe() {
  zrbte_sentinel

  local z_role="${BURD_TOKEN_3:-}"
  test -n "${z_role}" || buc_die "No role imprint — use tabtarget with imprint (e.g. rbtd-ap.AccessProbe.governor.sh)"

  local z_iterations=5
  local z_delay_ms=1500

input.description: Verify Theurge engine exits nonzero on case fail

content.text:
Yes — the existing pattern delivers exactly that semantic for free. No new mechanism needed.

### How it works end-to-end

**1. Theurge engine** (`rbtdre_engine.rs:130-160`) iterates all cases in a section, accumulates `passed`/`failed`/`skipped`. Default fail-soft per case — every handbook gets exercised regardless of which ones fail.

**2. Theurge main** (`main.rs:193-198`):
```rust
if result.failed > 0 {
    eprintln!("rbtd: {} case(s) failed", result.failed);
    ExitCode::FAILURE
} else {
    ExitCode::SUCCESS
}
```
Process exit: `0` iff every case passed.

**3. Bash wrapper** `rbte_run` (`rbte_engine.sh:106-116`) execs the binary under `set -e`:
```bash
"${ZRBTE_BINARY}" "${ZRBTE_FULL_MANIFEST}" "${z_fixture}"
```
Non-zero binary exit → bash dies → propagates up.

**4. Tabtarget** runs through dispatch → workbench → CLI, all under `set -euo pipefail`. Non-zero anywhere bubbles to the tabtarget's process exit.

### The single tabtarget

```bash
# tt/rbtd-r.Run.handbook-render.sh
#!/bin/bash
export BURD_LAUNCHER=".buk/launcher.rbtw_workbench.sh"
exec "${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}/../${BURD_LAUNCHER}" "${0##*/}" "${@}"
```

Identical pattern to existing `tt/rbtd-r.Run.regime-smoke.sh` — only the imprint changes. The `handbook-render` token (TOKEN_3) tells `rbte_run` which fixture to invoke; theurge runs all 16 cases; exit code is 0 iff all pass; the tabtarget's `$?` is exactly that.

### Total wire-up checklist for the all-handbooks tabtarget

1. **`rbtdrm_manifest.rs`** — 16 `pub const RBTDRM_COLOPHON_HANDBOOK_*: &str` declarations + `pub const RBTDRM_FIXTURE_HANDBOOK_RENDER: &str = "handbook-render"` + extend `rbtdrm_required_colophons` arm.
2. **New `rbtdrf_handbook.rs`** (or extend `rbtdrf_fast.rs`) — 16 case structs, each shells out to its tabtarget, `rbtdre_Verdict::Pass` iff exit 0. Section assembled and exposed similarly to `RBTDRF_SECTIONS_REGIME_SMOKE`.
3. **`rbtdrc_crucible.rs`** dispatch arm — add `RBTDRM_FIXTURE_HANDBOOK_RENDER => RBTDRF_SECTIONS_HANDBOOK_RENDER` to the section selector.
4. **`tt/rbtd-r.Run.handbook-render.sh`** — copy any existing `rbtd-r.Run.*.sh` byte-for-byte (the imprint comes from the filename).
5. **Optional: extend the fast suite** — add `"handbook-render"` to `ZRBTE_SUITE_FAST` array in `rbte_engine.sh` (so it runs alongside the existing fast fixtures) AND/OR leave it standalone via the dedicated tabtarget.

The user-visible "run all handbooks, exit 0 iff all pass" tabtarget is item 4 alone. Items 1-3 register the fixture; item 5 is wiring choice.

### CI invocation

```bash
$ tt/rbtd-r.Run.handbook-render.sh
$ echo $?    # 0 iff every handbook rendered without error
```

That's the contract you want — and it's expressible with no new framework, just enrolling one more fixture in Theurge's existing case engine.

Want me to:
1. Wrap ₢A6AAn (since the original docket no longer reflects the work)
2. Enroll a fresh pace scoped to "handbook-render fixture + baseline run + repair findings"
3. Then implement?

Or keep ₢A6AAn and reslate? Either is fine — your call on history hygiene.

message.content: Does this concept fully resolve the issues raised in A6AAn?

content.text:
No. The handbook-render fixture is **infrastructure** that detects handbook crashes; it doesn't perform any of the original repair work. Concretely:

| Original ₢A6AAn item | Resolved by fixture? |
|---|---|
| **Crash fix** — rbhw0_top.sh line 52-53 JJZ unbound var | **No.** Fixture would *detect* it (the `rbw-hw` case would fail), but the fix itself is separate work. |
| **Hardcoded `tt/jjw-tfP2...{host}.sh`** on line 53 | **No.** Cosmetic/architectural; invisible to "does it exit 0." |
| **Triple `buyy_cmd_yawp` capture** dedup (lines 34, 46, 58) | **No.** Cosmetic; the duplicates work, just wastefully. |
| **Furnish hygiene** — unused `buv_validation.sh`/`rbcc_Constants.sh`/`rbgc_Constants.sh` in rbhw0_cli.sh | **No.** Sourcing dead deps doesn't fail; fixture would render fine even with the cruft. |

What the fixture *does* give us that the original docket implicitly wanted:

- **Verification step** ("Run `tt/rbw-hw.Windows.sh` ... confirm it doesn't crash on the JJZ line") — promoted from one-off manual check to durable automated assertion.
- **Confidence that other handbooks aren't silently broken** — the original docket scoped to Windows; the fixture sweeps everything and may surface analogous bugs in the onboarding or payor families.
- **Regression net for future handbook work** — once we fix the four issues, the fixture catches future regressions.

### Honest scope picture

The conversation moved us from "small targeted fix" to "build the detector first, then fix from a position of knowledge." That's a real scope shift — the original ₢A6AAn was rough/critical-fix; the new direction is infrastructure-first with repairs as downstream paces.

Suggested pace structure:

- **This pace (whatever its identity)** — build handbook-render fixture + run baseline + commit findings to paddock.
- **Follow-up pace(s)** — actual repairs informed by the baseline: definitely the JJZ crash, plus the three Windows cleanups, plus anything else the baseline surfaces.

That keeps each pace honest about what it accomplished, and the order matches the discovery: infrastructure, observation, repair.

I lean toward **wrap ₢A6AAn unwrapped (no work landed) and enroll a fresh pace** for the fixture work — the original docket's "Character: Small, critical fix" doesn't fit anymore, and reslating to "build a Rust test fixture" feels like rewriting history rather than recording what actually happened. Cleaner to mark ₢A6AAn as a thread that opened a different door.

Want me to do that, or keep going with reslate?

message.content: I'm musing slating a new first pace in heat to implement this now, and reslate A6AAn given that work.  Thoughts?

content.text:
Like it. Cleaner than my "wrap empty + fresh pace" suggestion because it preserves the spook context — we discovered a crash, recognized we couldn't fix it well without a detector, built the detector, then fixed (informed by what the detector revealed). The original AAn coronet stays as the repair pace; its slot just shifts from "first" to "second."

Three small refinements to consider:

### 1. Sequencing within the new pace

The new pace's docket should produce a tangible artifact for the reslated AAn to consume. Two flavors:

- **(a)** New pace scope = "build fixture only." Closes when fixture compiles and is wired in. The first run + findings happens at the start of AAn's repair work.
- **(b)** New pace scope = "build fixture + run baseline + record findings to paddock." Closes with documented findings in the paddock. AAn then has a concrete repair list.

I lean **(b)** — it leaves AAn with a clear shopping list rather than starting AAn with an exploration step. Also gives a natural pause point (you review the baseline before authorizing repairs).

### 2. AAn's reslated docket — write now or write later

Two options:

- **Write now**: placeholder docket like *"Repair handbook crashes revealed by ₢A6AA{new}'s baseline. Docket to be refined with concrete list once baseline runs."* — sets intent, deferred specifics.
- **Write later**: leave AAn's docket as-is for now; reslate AAn with the concrete repair list after the new pace closes and we know what's broken.

I lean **write later**. The current AAn docket (windows crash + cleanup) becomes accurate AS A SUBSET if the baseline confirms only Windows is sick; if other handbooks are sick too, the docket gets honest expansion. Either way you reslate from real evidence rather than imagined scope.

### 3. New pace silks

Suggest `handbook-render-fixture-and-baseline` or similar — explicit about both halves of the work (build the fixture, observe initial state). Assignment comes from `jjx_enroll`; coronet system-assigned.

### Concrete next moves

1. Mass-reslate paddock: add brief Implementation Progress note about handbook-render fixture as a new infrastructure track, just so the paddock reflects this design conversation.
2. `jjx_enroll` new pace as `first` (ahead of AAn), with full docket for the fixture-build + baseline-run.
3. Implement and run baseline.
4. After baseline runs, decide on AAn reslate based on findings.

Sound right? If yes, want me to:

input.content:
# jjezs_slate handbook-render-fixture

## Character
Infrastructure build. Pure addition — new Rust fixture in Theurge plus a tabtarget. No production bash touched. Lands durable regression coverage for every handbook display before any repair work begins. Cognitive posture: mechanical execution against the design crystallized in the predecessor design conversation; pattern is well-established (mirror existing fast-suite fixtures).

## Docket
Add a `handbook-render` fixture to Theurge that exercises every handbook display tabtarget and reports per-case pass/fail. Wire into the fast suite. Run the baseline; communicate per-handbook results to the user; those results inform the reslated `₢A6AAn`'s ceiling.

### Implementation
- **`Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdrm_manifest.rs`** — add `pub const RBTDRM_COLOPHON_*` for each of the 16 handbook colophons (one symbolic ref per `RBZ_*` zipper constant); add `pub const RBTDRM_FIXTURE_HANDBOOK_RENDER: &str = "handbook-render"`; extend `rbtdrm_required_colophons()` arm so manifest verification covers the new fixture.
- **`Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdrf_handbook.rs`** (new file) — one case per colophon. Each case shells out to its tabtarget (via the existing `rbtdrf_run_bash` pattern or equivalent) and returns `Verdict::Pass` iff exit 0. Section assembled and exposed (e.g. `RBTDRF_SECTIONS_HANDBOOK_RENDER`) parallel to `RBTDRF_SECTIONS_REGIME_SMOKE`.
- **`Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdrc_crucible.rs`** dispatch arm — add `RBTDRM_FIXTURE_HANDBOOK_RENDER => crate::rbtdrf_handbook::RBTDRF_SECTIONS_HANDBOOK_RENDER` to the section selector match.
- **`Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/lib.rs`** — register the new module (`pub mod rbtdrf_handbook;`).
- **`tt/rbtd-r.Run.handbook-render.sh`** (new tabtarget) — byte-for-byte copy of any existing `rbtd-r.Run.*.sh`; imprint `handbook-render` comes from the filename.
- **`Tools/rbk/rbtd/rbte_engine.sh`** — add `"handbook-render"` to `ZRBTE_SUITE_FAST` so the fast suite includes it. Standalone tabtarget invocation continues to work independently.

### Covered colophons (sync discipline — when adding handbooks to rbz_zipper.sh, add a const here)
- Onboarding (8): `RBZ_ONBOARD_START_HERE`, `RBZ_ONBOARD_CRASH_COURSE`, `RBZ_ONBOARD_CRED_RETRIEVER`, `RBZ_ONBOARD_CRED_DIRECTOR`, `RBZ_ONBOARD_FIRST_CRUCIBLE`, `RBZ_ONBOARD_DIR_FIRST_BUILD`, `RBZ_ONBOARD_PAYOR_HB`, `RBZ_ONBOARD_GOVERNOR_HB`
- Windows (5): `RBZ_HANDBOOK_TOP`, `RBZ_HANDBOOK_WINDOWS`, `RBZ_HW_DOCKER_DESKTOP`, `RBZ_HW_DOCKER_WSL_NATIVE`, `RBZ_HW_DOCKER_CONTEXT`
- Payor (3): `RBZ_PAYOR_ESTABLISH`, `RBZ_PAYOR_REFRESH`, `RBZ_QUOTA_BUILD`

### Sync mechanism (free, already-existing pattern)
Bash `rbz_zipper.sh:179` exports `ZRBZ_COLOPHON_MANIFEST` (space-separated colophon string). Theurge's `rbtdrm_verify` checks each `RBTDRM_COLOPHON_*` const value appears in that manifest at startup. Renaming a colophon in `rbz_zipper.sh` without updating the corresponding Rust const causes loud failure at theurge startup — no silent drift.

### Baseline run + report
After fixture builds and wires:
- Run `tt/rbtd-r.Run.handbook-render.sh` standalone
- Capture per-case pass/fail
- Report results to the user; those results populate the "Baseline ceiling" section of reslated `₢A6AAn`'s docket

### Wrap criterion
- Fixture compiles
- Theurge's `rbtdrm_verify` passes for handbook-render fixture
- Standalone tabtarget runs and reports per-case results
- Fast suite continues to run (handbook-render included alongside enrollment-validation, regime-validation, regime-smoke)
- Non-zero exit due to known JJZ crash is **expected and not a wrap blocker** — the fixture doing its job IS the wrap criterion, not all cases passing
- Per-handbook results communicated to user
